title: Reconcile Meshtastic compatibility target architecture
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN has Python and Rust Meshtastic compatibility prototypes plus docs, while the active MVP Beads require a Zephyr-side firmware adapter. Current local schemas and transports do not cleanly map to Zephyr C/nanopb, Rust/FFI, or a host-side bridge. What needs to be done: decide and document whether the MVP implementation target is Zephyr C/nanopb, Rust generated code behind an FFI boundary, a host/client bridge, or a staged combination; update dependencies and docs accordingly. Acceptance criteria: selected architecture is recorded in Beads notes/design; implementation Beads point to the selected code path; docs no longer claim native Zephyr is unsupported if Zephyr remains the MVP target; unsupported paths are explicitly deferred.
notes:
2026-06-30 architecture decision for Meshtastic compatibility MVP: implement the firmware path natively in Zephyr C as a small optional subsystem/app component, using a bounded no-dynamic-allocation protobuf subset codec. The adapter is a Zephyr-side local app compatibility surface, not a Rust FFI feature and not a Python/host bridge.

Rationale:
- Active MVP Beads require Zephyr firmware behavior on boards such as R1/R1 Neo/T-Echo/T-Deck; app discovery and BLE GATT must run on the node.
- Current Zephyr tree already uses C Kconfig/CMake subsystems and app-level modules under lichen/subsys/lichen and lichen/apps/gateway. Existing local BLE code is Zephyr C (gateway/src/ble_uart.c) using BT_GATT_SERVICE_DEFINE and bounded buffers.
- Current native LICHEN protocol is Zephyr C (lichen/lib/native) with fixed buffers and compact codec helpers; this is the local pattern for firmware app protocols.
- Rust lichen-meshtastic is useful as schema/reference/host tooling but currently uses prost/prost-build, alloc/String/Vec assumptions, generated Rust, and no Zephyr C build integration or FFI boundary. It also has BLE length-header assumptions that conflict with current Meshtastic BLE raw-protobuf semantics.
- Python adapter is development/simulation only and currently does not parse/dispatch ToRadio, so it cannot satisfy unmodified mobile app firmware behavior.
- A host bridge would not make a BLE phone discover/connect to the node as Meshtastic and would not satisfy the firmware MVP.

Selected implementation shape:
- Add/extend a Zephyr-side Meshtastic compatibility module, expected under lichen/subsys/lichen/meshtastic/ or a closely scoped gateway app component until the subsystem boundary stabilizes.
- Add Kconfig options for the adapter, codec, BLE GATT transport, fixed buffer sizes, queue depth, synthetic metadata policy, and optional diagnostic LogRadio.
- Use source-provenanced Meshtastic protobuf subset from meshtastic/protobufs 032b7dfd68e875c4323e6ac67590c6fc616b1714 and generated or checked-in bounded C codec code chosen by project-LICHEN-t2hn.3.
- BLE transport must expose Meshtastic service/ToRadio/FromRadio/FromNum UUIDs as raw protobuf GATT values. It must not reuse NUS UUIDs or serial/TCP 0x94 0xc3 length framing for BLE.
- Adapter maps ToRadio/FromRadio to the shared LICHEN app-level behavioral contract, then to native CoAP/local message interfaces where available. Meshtastic compatibility must not enter the LICHEN RF path as Meshtastic packets or imply RF interoperability.
- Keep Rust/Python as reference/tooling/test clients; do not link Rust into Zephyr firmware for MVP unless a later Bead explicitly selects and proves a Zephyr-safe FFI path.

Recommended first firmware boards from architecture review: start on t_echo_nrf52840 or r1_neo_nrf52840 because current gateway board configs already enable BLE plus LoRa for nRF52840/SX1262-class hardware and gateway main already has optional BLE initialization. Avoid STM32WL for this MVP because it has no BLE path and tight memory. Treat ESP32-S3/T-Deck BLE Meshtastic compatibility as later validation because current T-Deck configs do not enable BLE local-client and ESP32-S3 Zephyr BLE support still needs board-level proof.

Docs updated in docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md: removed stale 'Native Zephyr not supported / use Rust via FFI' statement, documented Zephyr C as firmware MVP target, clarified Rust/Python as reference/tooling, corrected BLE raw-protobuf connection flow, replaced the broad 512-byte MTU claim with bounded-size/ATT follow-up language, and updated implementation file layout to point at a future Zephyr meshtastic module.

Implementation Bead implications:
- project-LICHEN-t2hn.3: choose/add Zephyr C bounded protobuf codec; Rust prost schema can be reference only.
- project-LICHEN-t2hn.4: implement Meshtastic BLE GATT in Zephyr C; do not adapt NUS as Meshtastic BLE.
- project-LICHEN-t2hn.5: implement raw-protobuf BLE dispatch plus bounded output queue; serial/TCP framing, if later supported, is a separate transport mode.
- project-LICHEN-t2hn.10: native_sim harness should drive the Zephyr C codec/dispatcher directly and can use Python/Rust only as golden-vector generators.

Review outcomes for doc change:
- Review pass 1 (Beauvoir): found missing heartbeat handling and missing region_presets/safe-absence policy. Fixed by documenting heartbeat in connection flow/inbound messages and adding region_presets to FromRadio outputs with safe-absence language.
- Review pass 2 (Mill): found invalid Rust feature command, overbroad Rust reference wording despite stale gatt.rs framing, and future subsystem path presented as current. Fixed by changing command to cargo build -p lichen-meshtastic, marking rust/lichen-meshtastic gatt.rs non-authoritative/stale for BLE framing, and renaming layout to Planned Firmware Files with Kconfig/CMake/include entries.
- Review pass 3 (Planck): found delivery receipts incorrectly mapped to CoAP ACK, heartbeat wording that implied pre-stage-1 heartbeat requirement, and wholesale Admin rejection despite read-only owner/session app behavior. Fixed by requiring app-visible ROUTING_APP ACK/NAK with decoded.request_id, changing heartbeat to tolerate but not require before stage 1, and separating admin config writes from read-only owner/session requests.

Verification: ruby line-length check for docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md found no >200-character lines after fixes; git diff --check passed; targeted grep found no stale 'Native Zephyr unsupported', 'Rust via FFI', nonexistent meshtastic-compat feature, or 512-byte MTU claims.

Remaining architecture risks tracked elsewhere: payload/ATT size contract in project-LICHEN-t2hn.18, exchange vectors in project-LICHEN-t2hn.19, synthetic metadata policy in project-LICHEN-t2hn.15, current app sync contract in project-LICHEN-t2hn.13. Architecture review by Halley had no contradictory recommendation; it added first-board guidance and the same blocker list already covered by t2hn.3/t2hn.4/t2hn.10/t2hn.18/t2hn.19.
close_reason: Selected Zephyr C bounded-codec firmware architecture for Meshtastic compatibility MVP; updated docs and recorded three review passes with fixes.
